specific_mechanism: OST-Stop: construct e-process E_n over partial decode tokens; stop when E_n > 1/α (anytime-valid α-confidence). Compatible with arbitrary decode strategy via Ville's inequality.
llm_application: OST-Stop: at each decode step n, compute multiplier B_n based on token's normalized log-prob relative to baseline distribution (e.g., backbone model's distribution). E_n = E_{n-1} · B_n with E_0 = 1. By Ville's inequality, P(sup_n E_n ≥ 1/α) ≤ α under null. Stop decoding when E_n > 1/α; that triggers 'sufficient evidence' verdict with anytime-valid confidence.
novelty_claim: Martingale Foresight Sampling (2601.15482) uses Doob/OST/Martingale for reasoning paths; Anytime-Valid Sufficiency (2510.06478) for LLM generation. OST-Stop's specific e-process construction may overlap heavily.
motivation_justification: OST theorem is rigorous; martingale construction from generation log-probs needs approximation; structural transfer.
